title: Research on Medical Text Information Fusion and Cerebral Infarction Auxiliary Diagnosis Based on Large Language Models
authors: Desheng Liu, Zilong Chen, Sen Ji, Xuyang Zheng, Xiaobing Zhang, Zihan Zhou, Yunxiang Zhang, Guangyao Sun
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: Cerebral infarction is a neurological disorder with high incidence and disability rates, where early recognition and diagnosis are crucial for reducing mortality. Traditional prediction models primarily rely on imaging analysis or single diagnostic indicators, struggling to fully leverage semantic information within medical records and imaging reports. This paper proposes TextFusion-QwenStroke, a text-based multi-source fusion framework. This model achieves auxiliary diagnosis of cerebral infarction through fine-tuning on Qwen2.5-VL-3B. By integrating structured diagnostic data, unstructured medical records, and imaging report texts, the model enables risk prediction without relying on raw imaging data. A multi-layer Transformer architecture achieves text feature fusion, while a lightweight semantic alignment loss function ensures multi-source data consistency. Using the ‘stroke-risk-prediction-dataset’ from Kaggle and hospital-collected datasets, the model achieved an AUC of 0.901 and an F1 score of 0.872, significantly outperforming both logistic regression and unimodal language models. These results demonstrate the broad potential of large language models for disease prediction in scenarios involving medical text semantic understanding and where imaging examinations are unavailable.

title: Distribution-Agnostic Idle-Age-Aware Joint Channel and Packet-Size Learning in Cognitive Radio Networks
authors: Haythem Bany Salameh, Malik Umar
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: Cognitive radio (CR) enables opportunistic spectrum access while protecting primary users (PUs). This opportunistic transmission is uncertain due to time-varying PU activity (unpredictable PU returns) and heterogeneous channel quality. PU idle times differ across channels, in which some have long mean availability, others only short opportunities, and PU returns vary in severity. In multi-user CR networks (CRNs), contending CR users must coordinate over a control channel to reserve an idle PU channel and select a packet size that maximizes throughput while reducing PU-return-induced forced terminations. Most existing designs optimize channel assignment or packet sizing in isolation, assume memoryless PU idle times, and often ignore control contention/reservation overhead that directly reduces the residual transmission time. This paper investigates joint channel assignment and packet-size control under unknown PU availability while explicitly accounting for realistic control overhead time. We model the Residual PU-idle Time with Idle Age using a distribution-agnostic residual-life survival function and incorporate control overhead into the packet probability of success (PoS). We formulate a maximum-profit, discounted-return problem that maximizes long-term network goodput subject to a required PoS, a received signal-to-noise (SNR) threshold, and exclusive channel occupancy. Due to the uncertainty in PU activity and the long-term nature of the optimization problem, which cannot be solved using conventional optimization methods, we model it as a constrained Markov decision process (CMDP). To solve the resulting CMDP, we propose an on-policy State-Action-Reward-State-Action (SARSA) reinforcement learning (RL) algorithm with convergence-based exploration and a quantized/pruned action-space mechanism that filters infeasible channel–packet-size pairs to reduce online search complexity. The proposed RL-based algorithm learns perchannel PU dynamics online and jointly selects the throughput-maximizing channel and packet size. Our method is a hybrid model-assisted/model-free design, in which online survival/quantile estimates are used only for PoS-based feasibility screening, while SARSA learns the long-term policy directly from interaction without requiring a parametric PU model. Simulations under heterogeneous PU availability/severity show up to 32% throughput improvement, with fewer retransmission attempts than reference schemes.

title: Towards Heterogeneous Multimodal Sentiment Analysis with ASR Errors via Multi-Granularity Contrastive Coordination_supp1-3702501.pdf
authors: Zhiqiong Wang
abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis improves the understanding of human emotions by integrating multiple modalities. However, the language modality accessed by the actually deployed MSA systems is obtained through automatic speech recognition (ASR), which can induce text errors and lead to MSA performance degradation. Besides, multimodal heterogeneity and multimodal information imbalance further aggravate the negative impact of ASR errors. To this end, we propose a multi-granularity contrastive coordination framework (MGCCF) for achieving robust MSA under ASR errors. Specifically, an error sentiment awareness calibration (ESAC) unit is devised to learn cross-modal word-level correlation and contextual information, enabling the detection and calibration of erroneous sentiment word embedding. Furthermore, a multimodal contrastive coordination (MCC) paradigm is presented to achieve cross-modal multi-granularity alignment from both global semantic and local temporal aspects. Meanwhile, MCC ingeniously leverages non-verbal features to reinforce the ability of ESAC to correct anomalous sentiment information and refine the expression of language features. Eventually, a multimodal information fusion (MIF) approach is developed to deal with multimodal information imbalance, which interpretably quantifies the contributions of different modalities and achieves multimodal dynamic fusion. Comprehensive experiments on seven MSA datasets demonstrate that our MGCCF achieves remarkable performance and surpasses the current state-of-the-art MSA methods.

title: Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access Based M -Ary CDMA Systems
authors: Ruofei Ma, Huei-Hong Tsai, Hsiao-Hwa Chen
abstract: Code division multiple access (CDMA) is missing in 4G and 5G systems, albeit it possesses many salient technical advantages over OFDMA, such as its unique multipath diversity gain with RAKE receiver. This work aims to propose a way to vitalize the performance of CDMA systems with M-ary code modulation (i.e., M-ary CDMA). In particular, non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) technique is leveraged to augment spectrum efficiency. Built upon the foundation of a high spectrum efficiency of M-ary CDMA systems, NOMA is integrated with M-ary CDMA for enhancing its user capacity further. Specifically, this work showcases the designs of downlink transmitter and receiver structures for a NOMA based M-ary CDMA system, and validates the effectiveness of the proposed scheme in terms of improved user capacity and bit error rate via simulations. Finally, the challenges and limitations faced by the proposed scheme are identified and the directions for future research are given.

title: Vehicle-Side Lightweight Conditional Anonymous Double Authentication Framework with Password Robustness in VANETs
authors: Yangfan Liang, Zhiguo Wan, Yiming Chen, Zhiqiang Zhao, Gao Liu, Zhiquan Liu, Hong Sun, Yining Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: The emergence of Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) has revolutionized modern transportation by enabling real-time and cooperative communication between vehicles and infrastructure. However, vehicular interactions operate across two layers: human–vehicle access and vehicle–network communication. This dual-layered nature introduces complex security and privacy challenges. Existing Conditional Privacy-Preserving Authentication (CPPA) schemes mainly focus on message-level anonymity and integrity. However, they often overlook systemic vulnerabilities across the entire trust lifecycle. These limitations include the inability to verify driver legitimacy during vehicle access, high computational overhead due to heavyweight cryptographic primitives, the lack of password update and recovery mechanisms, and insufficient resistance to physical key extraction attacks. To address these issues, we propose VLCADA, a Vehicle-side Lightweight Conditional Anonymous Double Authentication framework. VLCADA establishes a vertically integrated security architecture for intelligent vehicles. It unifies multi-factor driver authentication with efficient and conditionally anonymous message authentication. At the access layer, VLCADA combines identity–password credentials, biometric verification, and Physical Unclonable Functions (PUF). This ensures that only legitimate users can access and control the vehicle. At the communication layer, we design a Signer-side Lightweight Conditional Anonymous Signature (SLCAS) scheme. This scheme shifts computationally intensive operations to an offline phase, leaving only lightweight hash and XOR operations during real-time execution. In addition, we propose a Dynamic Password Mechanism (DPM) based on threshold secret sharing. This mechanism enables secure password update and recovery without trusted authority intervention. Furthermore, by integrating PUF with fuzzy extractors, VLCADA effectively mitigates noise and provides resistance against physical attacks. Comprehensive security analysis shows that VLCADA satisfies essential security requirements. Experimental results demonstrate that VLCADA reduces total computational overhead by up to 48.55% and communication cost by up to 32.61% compared with state-of-the-art schemes.

title: A Compact 132.5–142-GHz ×6 Frequency Multiplier With 2.4-dBm Output Power and 2.1% DC-RF Efficiency in 65-nm CMOS
authors: Yi Wu, Zhe Yang, Zongyang Zhang, Dujie He, Guangyin Feng, Fanyi Meng, Xiu Yin Zhang
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a compact D-band <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\times 6$</tex-math> </inline-formula> frequency multiplier, which consists of a self-mixing tripler, a driver amplifier (DA), a push–push doubler, and a D-band power amplifier (PA). A self-mixing technique is introduced in the tripler to generate the third harmonic with a harmonic-selective matching network codesigned at its output. This approach consequently reduces the generation of spurious signals near the sixth harmonic frequency. A prototype was implemented in a 65-nm CMOS process with a compact area of only 0.22 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. Measurement results show that it achieves an output frequency range of 132.5–142 GHz, a saturated output power of 2.4 dBm, a maximum DC-RF efficiency of 2.1%, and a harmonic rejection ratio (HRR) of 36 dBc at 137 GHz.

title: Dispatch Strategy Against Typhoon-Induced Wind Farm Cut-Outs Considering Resilience: An Adaptive Robust Stochastic Optimization Approach
authors: Haitao Xu, Haotian Zhao, Hongbin Sun, Qinglai Guo
abstract: Typhoon disasters pose serious threats to the secure operation of wind-integrated power systems by damaging transmission lines and perturbing wind power output. To address the typhoon-induced large-scale unplanned wind farm cut-outs, it is necessary to develop an orderly wind curtailment plan in day-ahead dispatch. However, balancing robustness and wind power utilization in such plans remains challenging. Furthermore, it is difficult for a single uncertain optimization framework to simultaneously account for the distinct uncertainties in both transmission contingency and wind power output, leading to strategies with limited practicality. To tackle these challenges, this paper proposes a day-ahead dispatch model incorporating an orderly wind curtailment strategy, using adaptive robust stochastic optimization (ARSO) to handle the dual uncertainties of line failure and wind generation. The model consists of pre-dispatch and re-dispatch stages. The curtailment plan is determined in the form of curtailment thresholds in pre-dispatch stage and implemented in re-dispatch stage. In addition, this paper designs a wind power sample generation method and develops a solution algorithm for the ARSO model based on the column-and-constraint generation (C&CG) approach. Case studies on modified IEEE 39-bus and 118-bus systems verify the necessity of orderly wind curtailment and the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Enhancing Representation Inversion and Alignment for Zero-Shot Composed Image Retrieval
authors: Feifei Zhang, Jia Chen, Haoliang Zhou, Changsheng Xu
abstract: Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) retrieves a target image by combining a reference image with a textual modification. Existing supervised methods have demonstrated strong performance; however, their dependence on costly, manually annotated triplets limits their scalability and generalizability across diverse applications. Zero-Shot Composed Image Retrieval (ZS-CIR) addresses this by mapping visual inputs to textual representations, enabling annotation-free composed image retrieval. However, accurately capturing both fine-grained details and high-level semantics remains a key challenge. To address this, we introduce an enhanced visual representation inversion and alignment model designed to bridge the gap between visual and textual modalities. Specifically, we propose a Multi-Level Visual Prompting (MLVP) module that integrates local image information into global representations through both instance-level and feature-level prompting, ensuring a more comprehensive capture of fundamental visual content. We further introduce a Dynamic Alignment (DA) module that complements contrastive learning, ensuring semantic flow is effectively transferred and aligned across different modalities without adding extra trainable parameters, thereby narrowing the semantic gap between the visual and textual representations. Extensive experiments across multiple datasets validate the superiority of our method over existing state-of-the-art approaches, demonstrating robust generalization and adaptability for zero-shot composed image retrieval.

title: Unique Ferroxplana Ferrites Impedance Matched to Free Space at HF to VHF Frequencies and Beyond
authors: Van Dao Nguyen, Coenradt Taylor, Katherine A. Kelly, Thi Thuy Pham, Ying Chen, Ogheneyunume Fitchorova, Vincent G. Harris
abstract: new class of RF magnetoceramic materials has been developed for operation across the high-frequency (HF), very-high-frequency (VHF) and lower ultra-high-frequency UHF regime, spanning approximately 1 MHz ≤ f ≤ 1000 MHz. These materials are realized as Ztype ferroxplana ferrites with nominal compositions Ba₁.₅Sr₁.₅Co₂−xMₓFe₂₄O₄₁, where M = Mn or Zn and x = 0, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, and 1.25. Through compositional substitution and microstructural engineering, the electromagnetic constitutive parameters of these materials can be tailored such that the effective magnetic permeability (μᵣ) and dielectric permittivity (εᵣ) are approximately equal over broad frequency ranges. Under these conditions the normalized impedance approaches that of free space (Z₀ ≈ 377 Ω), enabling efficient electromagnetic coupling with minimal reflection at the material interface. Magnetodielectrics satisfying this impedance-matched condition represent an important materials platform for advanced RF technologies, including miniaturized antennas, radomes, electromagnetic absorbers, and broadband RF components. Because electromagnetic waves can enter such media without significant impedance discontinuities, device architectures can be designed with reduced reflection losses while simultaneously exploiting the high refractive index associated with large μᵣεᵣ products. This combination enables substantial reductions in electrical size, device thickness, and system complexity, while also enhancing bandwidth and radiation efficiency. Experimental characterization of these Z-type ferrites demonstrates operational frequency ranges extending from approximately 1 MHz to beyond 800 MHz, with usable bandwidths exceeding 500 MHz. The materials exhibit size-reduction factors (SRF) greater than 20, while maintaining relatively low loss parameters, with magnetic loss tangents on the order of tan δμ ≈ 0.01 and dielectric loss tangents typically below tan δε < 10⁻³. The simultaneous realization of broadband impedance matching, high magnetodielectric loading, and low loss is uncommon among presently available materials. Consequently, these systems represent a promising foundation for a new generation of impedance-engineered magnetodielectrics (ZEMs) capable of enabling compact, broadband RF devices for communications, sensing, and electromagnetic energy management.

title: Lightweight and Interpretable Deep Learning for Automated Waste Classification
authors: Saad Ahmed, Razia Sultana, Qazi Masrafi Rahman, Aroshi Ali, Khalid Syfullah
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The increasing volume of solid waste poses serious environmental and public health challenges, especially in rapidly urbanizing countries like Bangladesh. Effective waste classification plays a key role in improving recycling rates and minimizing pollution. In this study, we present a deep learningbased framework for automatic waste classification using an extended version of the BDWaste dataset. We evaluated the performance of four pretrained convolutional neural networks which are MobileNetV2, VGG16, DenseNet121 and Xception alongside a custom lightweight CNN designed for deployment in resource-constrained settings achieving very high accuracay of 98.76%. MobileNetV2 achieved the highest classification accuracy, while the custom CNN showed competitive performance with significantly lower computational requirements. To enhance interpretability, we applied the Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) technique, which offered visual insights into the model's decision-making process. This work supports the development of intelligent waste classification systems that contribute to urban sustainability and environmental protection efforts.

title: Escaping Stability-Plasticity Dilemma in Online Continual Learning for Motion Forecasting via Synergetic Memory Rehearsal
authors: Yunlong Lin, Chao Lu, Tongshuai Wu, Xiaocong Zhao, Guodong Du, Yanwei Sun, Zirui Li, Jianwei Gong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have achieved remarkable success in intelligent systems such as autonomous vehicles and robots. However, most DNN-based methods suffer from catastrophic forgetting, where the DNN may fail to maintain its performance in previously learned tasks after adapting to new data. This phenomenon gives rise to a critical tradeoff between memory stability of the DNN, that is, the ability to retain learned knowledge, and learning plasticity, that is, the capacity to acquire new information effectively. To address such stability–plasticity dilemma, this study proposes a novel CL method named synergetic memory rehearsal (SyReM). SyReM maintains a compact memory buffer to represent learned knowledge. To ensure memory stability, it employs an inequality constraint that limits increments in the average loss over the memory buffer. Synergistically, a selective memory rehearsal mechanism is designed to enhance learning plasticity by selecting samples from the memory buffer that are similar to recently observed data. This selection is based on an online-measured cosine similarity of loss gradients, ensuring targeted memory rehearsal. We validate SyReM under online CL tasks for motion forecasting. Comprehensive experiments on naturalistic driving datasets demonstrate that, compared to non-CL and CL baselines, SyReM significantly mitigates catastrophic forgetting in past scenarios while improving forecasting accuracy in new ones. The code is available at https://github.com/BIT-Jack/SyReM

title: Field Measurement-Based Modeling and Validation of Electric Arc Furnace Loads
authors: Firoz Ahmad, Mohammad Tayyab, S. M. Suhail Hussain, T. K. Abdel-Galil, Ibrahim El-Amin
abstract: Electric Arc Furnace (EAF) is an industrial load that generates large active and reactive power fluctuations, voltage flicker, and harmonic distortion at the point of common coupling. These characteristics present significant challenges to Power Quality (PQ) management and motivate the need for accurate, measurement-based EAF modeling. This paper presents a field measurement-based modeling and validation study of a large EAF installation connected to the Saudi Arabian power grid. This work uses field measurements collected at an operating industrial site, enabling comparison with existing studies. The measurements were conducted at multiple voltage levels in a large steel plant supplied at 230 kV in Saudi Arabia. One-week measurements at the 230 kV interface were used to evaluate voltage variation, flicker severity, and Voltage Total Harmonic Distortion (V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">THD</sub>), confirming compliance with IEEE Std. 519-2022. Additional synchronized recordings were collected at internal plant locations, including 34.5 kV buses before and after static volt-ampere reactive compensators and series reactors, and at the EAF transformer low-voltage terminals. The measured data were used to select, tune, and validate two EAF models. For the melting stage, the differential-equation model shows close agreement with measurements, with per phase voltage and current errors of 2.7% and 1.6%, respectively, and measured V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">THD</sub> of 5.5%, while the stochastic arc-length model gives per phase voltage and current errors of about 6.3%. These results support the use of the validated model for PQ and mitigation studies under the considered operating conditions.

title: A DC-DC Converter for Electric Vehicle Charging Station
authors: Fenglian Wang, Guibin Zou, Xiuyan Wei, Shengkun Pang
abstract: High voltage gain non-isolated dc-dc converters are finding an increasingly wide range of applications in clean energy generation systems. In applications, such as electric vehicle charging station, the low ripple input current of dc-dc converters can prolong its service life. Accordingly, this paper Improved and derived a dc-dc converters with low ripple input current and low voltage stress of switch. The converters are characterized by high efficiency, extra-high voltage gain, continuous input current, passive clamping, and common ground. It adopts a unique three-winding coupled-inductor and a switched capacitor to enhance its boost capability, and has three modulation indexes, i.e., the boost turn ratio (n1>1), the buck turn ratio (n2<1), and the duty cycle D. The introduction of buck turn ratio n2 not only avoids an increase in the capacity and size of the coupled-inductor, but also reduces the leakage inductance, total inductance, and core size of the coupled inductor, which in turn reduces the loss of the coupled inductor, and increases the efficiency of converter. This paper studies steady-state characteristics of the converter, deduces the current/voltage relationship for each component, analyses the efficiency of converter. Finally, the feasibility of the proposed converter is verified via experiments.

title: Data-Driven Psychological Stress Classification Among Higher Education Students: A Machine Learning Approach
authors: Ajit Sharma, Dhiman Sarma, Saikat Das Roky
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Psychological stress is a serious issue for students in higher education institutions, especially in Bangladesh. Many students deal with academic pressure, financial problems, and high social expectations. Although mental health issues are increasing, most institutions do not have effective ways to spot or address stress early. These challenges can harm students' academic results and overall well-being. There is a clear need for predictive models that consider personal stress factors like family expectations, financial struggles, and peer pressure. This study fills an important gap in research, since earlier studies have often missed the specific stressors students face in higher education or have not used a wide range of diverse data, like academic records, health information, behavior, and personal challenges. In this research paper, we propose a data-driven technique using machine learning (ML) algorithms to classify stress. To address this, we built a predictive model using 801 student records, with 80% used for training and 20% for testing. We tested five ML algorithms: Random Forest (RF), Naive Bayes (NB), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Logistic Regression (LR). The LR model performed best, reaching 94.56% accuracy and a macro Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 99.54. SVM came next with a slightly lower AUC. Random Forest had 74.15% accuracy, which may suggest overfitting. KNN and Naive Bayes achieved 67.35% and 76.19% accuracy, respectively. The work's potential to address the growing number of mental health issues among students by giving educational institutions a tool for predicting stress before it reaches critical levels makes it socially necessary. Early detection can improve student well-being and lower dropout rates. The model promotes inclusivity by considering socioeconomic factors, which makes it appropriate for a range of student demographics and enhances fairness in mental health support services.

title: Power MOSFETs With Super Safe-Operating-Area
authors: Mark A. Gajda, Ali Ghasemi, Hamza Patel
publish: 2026 IEEE 38th International Symposium on Power Semiconductor Devices and ICs (ISPSD)
abstract: In this work we present a method of eliminating the undesirable effect of premature thermal runaway in MOSFETs operating in linear-mode and show a fully fabricated 100V-class split-gate trench power-MOSFET that achieves the theoretical safe-operating-area entitlement with minimal impact on R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>. The mechanism responsible for thermal runaway is described in terms of competition between charge carrier density, device channel mobility and system geometry. A solution is proposed, which involves integrating two MOSFETs onto the same die to increase the average local thermal mass of active cells, thereby reducing the local thermal impedance. This allows for very fast thermal stabilisation of the device during linear-mode operation by inverting the sign of relevant temperature coefficients and quickly transitioning the device into negative feedback, thus preventing thermal runaway. The operating principles are electrothermal, hence universal, and can be transferred to any base technology, material, voltage rating, and package configuration.

title: A Transformer-Based Perspective to Bangla-English Code-Mixed Sentiment Analysis
authors: Sandwip Dash Talukder, Debajyoti Das, Mahmudul Hasan Badhan, Rubel Ahmod, Md Saidul Islam, Islam Md Saiful
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Despite Bangla being the seventh most spoken language worldwide, it remains a low-resource language in Natural Language Processing, particularly concerning Bangla-English (BE) code-mixed text. While Banglish (transliterated Bangla) has been studied, sentiment analysis of text containing native Bangla script alongside English words remains underexplored. This paper addresses this gap by introducing BE-CodeMix-3, a manually annotated sentiment dataset of 8,179 social media comments categorized into three sentiment classes. We provide a comprehensive evaluation across various architectures, including traditional Recurrent Neural Networks (LSTM, BiLSTM, GRU) and transformer-based models (mBERT, XLMRoBERTa, and BanglaBERT). Experimental results demonstrate that language-specific pretraining is critical for capturing the nuances of code-mixed text. The BanglaBERT model significantly outperforms all baselines, achieving a peak accuracy and F1-score of 0.90. Our findings highlight that while multilingual models provide a strong foundation, transformers pretrained specifically on large-scale Bangla corpora offer superior semantic and syntactic encoding for real-world, codemixed applications.

title: Investigation of SVD-Based Speech Emotion Recognition
authors: Divya Sharma, Kushalatha M R, H. Mallika, Garima Joshi
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: Over time, research activity in the domain of Speech Emotion Recognition (SER) is steadily pacing, utilizing Matlab and Python interfaces. It involves analyzing input speech to discern the underlying emotional state being conveyed. This process typically undergoes stages like feature extraction, feature matching, classification, and database integration. Emotions recognized by the system encompasses anger, worry, happiness, neutrality, and gloomy. Various models enable the analysis of specific characteristics within the speech signal, allowing the system identification of emotional state expressed. The main objective of this work is to present the implementation of the Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) algorithm in combination with varied classifiers and speech emotion databases for detecting human's emotions and evaluating the performance of SVD-SER with respect to different classifiers. The results show that SVD with CNN SER gives higher accuracy as compared to other classification methods. LSTM also exhibits good precision but gives more number of false negatives.

title: Enhancing Security in Multicasting Using Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface and Selection Combining Diversity Technique
authors: Shafi Abid, Md. Zahurul Islam Sarkar, Muhtasib Farhan, Ahshan Habib
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: This paper analyzes physical-layer secure multicasting in a wireless network that utilizes reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) utilizing selection combining (SC) diversity receivers. Although RIS enhances signal directionality, it is still vulnerable to deep fading. This work shows that the integration of SC provides a vital diversity advantage that RIS alone cannot attain. Hence it successfully mitigates deep fading in secure multicast scenarios. Analytical formulations in closed form have been established for the Probability Density Function (PDF), Cumulative Distribution Function (CDF), and Ergodic Secrecy Multicast Capacity (ESMC). The results confirm the study and demonstrate that the proposed RIS-assisted SC technique achieves significant enhancements in secrecy compared to the non-diversity system, especially at higher Signal-to-Noise Ratios (SNR). Moreover, the findings suggest that increasing the number of RIS reflecting elements improves secrecy performance, whereas an increased number of multicast users decreases ESMC due to the selection of the lowest SNR value at the destination user.

title: An Intelligent Student Management System Using Temporal Stability-Aware Adaptive Learning
authors: Aihui Nie, Zhixin Yao
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In modern times, student management systems played a critical role in modern educational institutions by allowing effectively handling of academic records, attendance tracking and student performance monitoring. In existing big data-driven decision tree-based student management platform is used to classify and monitor student performance and behavior. However, this existing approach struggled with overfitting which is caused by frequent learning updates and restricted scalability while assisting continuous real-time data that limited its efficiency in deployment. To overcome these challenges, Temporal Stability-Aware Adaptive Learning Layer (TSALL) is proposed for an improved student management system. Primarily, data is collected from multiple real-world sources and preprocessed to remove inconsistencies and data is normalized for ensuring reliability. Further, TSALL analyzes student data across many time periods for identifying stable behavior and patterns of performance. Here, learning updates are selectively triggered only when consistent patterns persist over time by avoiding unrequired model complexity and reduces computational overhead. Moreover, data is employed to classify student performance and update individual student's profiles in real-time. Finally, proposed TSALL-based student management system efficiently reduces model complexity by supporting real-time student data processing. Additionally, by learning only from stable student behavior patterns, proposed TSALL obtained better generalization and reliable performance. Therefore, proposed TSALL framework achieved better results in accuracy (95.1 %) and precision (97.4 %) compared to existing approach.

title: AI-Powered Hybrid Management Framework for Autonomous Vendor Evaluation and Procurement Optimization
authors: Mohyedin Hamza, M Swapna, Navdeep Singh, K Bhasakara Rao, Krishna Bhargavi Y, Kala Priydarshini G
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The optimization of procurement is an acute issue that enterprise organizations face working in unstable supply chains and dynamic regulatory environments. The old vendor management systems tend to be based on restrictive and rule-driven appraisals that just cannot evolve to non-linear and complex procurement world. This paper suggests employing AI-based hybrid management and uses rare and advanced algorithms such as a Quantum-Inspired Decision Matrix (QIDM), Swarm-Driven Contractual Risk Assessment (SDCRA), Graph-Based Trust Propagation Network (GTPN) and Evolutionary Procurement Policy Optimizer (EPPO). The model itself analyses vendors, identifies risks, advances procurement policies, and makes transparency available with blockchain-backed audit trails. The comparison of 50 procurement scenarios conducted through experimental analysis demonstrated that QIDM obtained a vendor ranking accuracy of 96, SDCRA had a risk detection precision of 93.3, and EPPO could allow saving up to 13.4 in procurement costs. Moreover, GTPN achieved a mean error of prediction of trust of only 2.65 which is better than conventional evaluation models. Additional support of multimodal sentiment analysis and neuro-fuzzy reinforcement decision layers further enhanced the profile of vendors and minimized conflict in decision by 47%. This model shows a sound, decipherable, and changeable approach to contemporary procurement issues. The next phase of work will involve the adoption of real-time IoT-built supply chains, including moral AI limits, and joint procurement through federated learning architecture.

title: Predicting Genre Crossovers in Book Recommendations Using Hybrid Machine Learning Models
authors: Estiyak Rubaiat, Jahir Uddin Mohammad Babar, Tojammel Jakaria Jenin, Mirza Fahim Abdullah, MD. Saif Al Shahriar, Sharfuddin Mahmood
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Traditional book recommendation systems struggle to predict genre crossover tendencies in modern books that frequently blend multiple genres, as they rely heavily on static metadata like genre tags and titles. This paper proposes a hybrid model that integrates sentiment analysis of user reviews, sequential pattern mining (via Apriori), and machine learning techniques-including XGBoost for feature-based prediction, Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) for modeling reading sequences-connected through a novel sentiment-guided structured hybrid integration layer. The approach captures emotional engagement and longterm user behavior to predict dynamic genre transitions. Experiments on a dataset of 10,872 books and over 52,000 ratings/reviews from Goodreads and Amazon show the proposed model achieves a top- 3 accuracy of 98.3% and an F1score of 78.6% (micro-averaged), significantly outperforming prior single-genre or non-hybrid methods. Ablation studies confirm the contribution of sentiment analysis and pattern mining. This work advances personalized, adaptive book recommendations by better handling genre fluidity in contemporary literature.

title: Research on Power Domain Anti-Jamming Methods Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: XingXiang Huang, Lin Qi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Applied Statistics, Modeling and Advanced Algorithms (ASMA)
abstract: With the intensifying confrontation between intelligent jamming and anti-jamming technologies and the continuous enhancement of system adaptability, both sides are adjusting their strategies in response to the other’s actions. This paper proposes the anti-jamming method LOLA and its improved version LOLA-DQN based on reinforcement learning in the context of power domain confrontation. Also, we employ game theory to describe agent-agent interactions.This algorithm gains a greater advantage in power confrontation among agents by learning the opponent’s gradient and combining it with its own learning network.

title: Energy-Efficient Green Computing Techniques for Edge Devices Using Attention-Guided Federated Learning
authors: Deepa Dawadi, S R V. Prasad Reddy, R. Deepa, S. Punitha, Aarthy. G
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In recent years, an energy efficient green computing methods for edge devices has gained attention because the rapid growth of intelligent Internet of Things (IoT) applications and the rising deployment of edge intelligence in resource needed environment. Whereas in such systems Federated Learning (FL) emerges as an effective pattern for distributed model training while storing data privacy. However, the existing Adaptive Decision Algorithm (ADA) method based on optimization driven resource allocation suffer additional computational and exhibit limited adaptability under dynamic workload and energy conditions. To address these issues, this research presents Federated Attention-Aware Network (FedAA-Net) a learning driven green computing framework was designed for edge devices operating under FL. Whereas, in this proposed system locally generated edge data is processed without centralized transmission and edge devices performs energy aware local training under the coordination of federated server. Then, an attention deep learning controller is embedded at every edge device to regulate computation and communication behavior by prioritizing low energy execution features. Next, this enables adaptive control of transmission scheduling, CPU utilization and federated participation without iterative optimization. Finally, the federated server aggregates lightweight model updates by using standard federated averaging, scalable coordination and providing privacy preservation. Experimental evaluation presents that the proposed framework reduced entire energy consumption and learning completion time while enhancing task level energy efficiency compared to the existing ADA model by confirming its effectiveness for sustainable edge intelligence.

title: A Comprehensive Hybrid Deep Learning Approach for Bone Fracture Detection in X-Ray Images Using Optimized CNNs and Transfer Learning
authors: Sumaya Hannan Shova, Rinku Islam, Sarwar Hossain, Md Ishtiaq Ahmed, Md. Abdul Malek, Mst. Mohona Akter, Sadekatul Shihab, Abdullah Al Bassam
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Bone fractures, particularly prevalent among elderly populations, demand rapid and precise diagnostic methods to mitigate complications and enable timely intervention. Tradi- tional fracture detection relying on radiologists' manual interpre- tation of X-ray images remains time-consuming and susceptible to human error, especially for subtle or complex fractures. This paper introduces a hybrid deep learning framework that integrates optimized convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with transfer learning to automate and enhance fracture detection in X-ray images. Utilizing the Human Bone Fracture Multi- modal Image Dataset (HBFMID), comprising diverse X-ray and MRI scans, our approach employs pre-trained CNN architectures (ResNet-101, DenseNet-121) fine-tuned via transfer learning to adapt to fracture-specific features, effectively addressing data scarcity challenges. Architectural optimizations including layer pruning, channelwise attention mechanisms, and adaptive pooling reduce computational complexity while improving sensitivity to critical fracture patterns. Augmentation techniques such as rotation, flipping, and noise injection further bolster model generalizability. Experimental results demonstrate state-of-theart performance, with the proposed model achieving the accuracy (ACC) of 95.66%, 99.41%, 97.70%, 99.67%, 99.41% and the value of AUC is 95.54%, 99.98%, 99.78%, 99.99%, 99.98% for Custom Classifier, SVM, RF, LR, XGBoost, respectively.

title: An Ensemble Learning-Based Artificial Intelligence Framework for Postpartum Depression and Suicidal Risk Prediction
authors: Tamanna Akter, Monir Hossen, Najnin Khan Nipa
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The birth of a baby brings significant emotional changes; however, many women experience psychological distress such as postpartum depression (PPD) and, in severe cases, suicidal behavior. PPD is a major public health concern that adversely affects maternal well-being and infant development. Early and reliable detection is therefore essential. This study proposes an ensemble learning-based framework for predicting PPD and suicidal risk using demographic and symptom-based data collected from 1,877 postpartum mothers. To address class imbalance, the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE) was applied. Several baseline machine learning classifiers, including Support Vector Machine, Naïve Bayes, Logistic Regression, and K-Nearest Neighbors, were evaluated and compared with ensemble models such as Random Forest, XGBoost, and an AdaBoost-Extra Trees hybrid. Model performance was assessed using 10-fold cross-validation and multiple train-test splits (60/40, 70/30, and 80/20), with the 70/30 split producing optimal results. Experimental findings indicate that ensemble models consistently outperform individual classifiers, achieving high predictive accuracy for suicidal risk (XGBoost: 94%) and postpartum depression (XGBoost: 95%). These results demonstrate the effectiveness of ensemble learning techniques for supporting early identification of high-risk postpartum women.

title: Dynamic Reinforcement Learning-Driven Event-Aware Adaptive for Education Management System
authors: Rui Guo, Meng Li, Ao Cui, Shulian Zhang, Guangsheng Cao, Qi Zhang
publish: 2026 3rd International Conference on Integrated Intelligence and Communication Systems (ICIICS)
abstract: In recent years, the rapid growth of online and blended learning environment has increased the need for intelligent Education Management Systems (EMS) capable of adapting to continuously evolving engagement patterns, learner behavior and academic progress. The existing Group-Theoretic Particle Swarm Optimization (GT-PSO) approach uses historical data to evaluate learner behavior but is rely on uses static decision rules and less capable of responding to dynamic events. To address these limitations, this research proposes a Dynamic Reinforcement Learning-driven Event-Aware Adaptive Management System (DREAM-EMS) for managing personalized and continuous educational delivery. Initially, learner interactions from the Open University Learning Analytics Dataset (OULAD) are modeled as an event-driven sequential decision-making process, while an online RL framework is employed to learn adaptive curriculum adjustment, intervention strategies and pacing modification in real-time. An Event-Triggered Adaptive Reward Reweighting (ET-ARR) mechanism dynamically adjusts optimization priorities based on detected learner events that enables timely engagement recovery, workload balancing and performance improvement. Finally, a closed-loop feedback mechanism continuously updates learner states and policies, which ensures stable and personalized adaptation over academic timelines to provide event-aware education management. The experimental results demonstrates that the proposed DREAM-EMS model achieved accuracy (97.8%), precision (95%) and recall (98%) compared to existing GT-PSO approach.

title: A Comparative Analysis of YOLO Variants with Improved YOLOv12m Model for Students' Classroom Behavior Detection
authors: Souvik Pramanik, Md. Ibrahim Khalil, Ornima Hafizur, Kamruddin Nur
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The automated detection of student classroom behavior is critical for enhancing engagement assessment and providing educators with real-time feedback. However, this task remains challenging owing to the prevalence of small, subtle, and often occluded actions. This study presents a robust framework for the real-time recognition of five key student behaviors: raising hands, using phones, bowing heads, bending, and turning heads. We conducted a comprehensive comparative analysis of state-of-the-art YOLO models, including YOLOv9m, YOLOv10m, YOLOv11m, and YOLOv12m. Our findings identified YOLOv12m as the most promising architecture in this domain. Through systematic fine-tuning and the- incorporation of advanced data augmentation, attention mechanisms, and hyperparameter optimization we significantly enhanced the YOLOv12m model. The resulting system achieved a state-of-the-art test mAP50 of 0.916, precision of 0.881, and recall of 0.861, outperforming all its baseline counterparts. Ablation studies confirmed the efficacy of multi-scale feature aggregation and attention modules in mitigating challenges related to scale and occlusion. The proposed framework demonstrated high robustness in real classroom scenarios, offering a practical and intelligent tool for automating behavioral analysis and supporting interactive teaching methods.
